PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-24-2024 Continued To Dec-03-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Joan Black (petitioner).

Mr. Wrobel stated that the letters of administration are needed to pursue the research into his case. The matter is continued to December 03, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Ng. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Continued To Sep-24-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for and with Joan Black (in person).
Deborah Fox (court appointed Guardian Ad Litem) appeared by zoon for John Black (intestate heir).

Mrs. Fox stated that circumstances appear to have changed, that she was brought in this case to address the issue of whether her client has capacity, that she believes he does not have capacity and that it is her understanding that limited authority is now being requested. Mr. Wrobel stated that they are looking into the issue of whether there is any real property, that getting a bond would be expensive to the estate, and that his client would like a 60-day continuance as she wants to file for conservatorship for her brother. Mrs. Fox asked to be discharged and stated that she is not seeking fees. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to September 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court will issue an order discharging Mrs. Fox as court appointed guardian ad litem for John Black.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-30-2024 Continued To Jul-30-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Adamsky appeared by zoom for and with Joan Black (petitioner) in person.
DeAndre Wright (Joan's son) also present in propria persona.

When asked by the court, Mr. Adamsky stated that he is not thoroughly familiar with the file. The court raised the issue of the signatures on the waivers of bond. Mrs. Black stated that John does not write, that he does a line or a circle and that he does not read. Mr. Wright stated that John is extremely cognitively disabled, spoke of John's medical condition, stated that Joan has been taking care of John, and that the first signature was from Joan and the second signature is a guided signature. Mr. Wright also described how they communicate with John/how they know when he understands something. Discussion was held regarding the purpose of a conservatorhsip and the authority that it will provide for making legal and medical decisions. The court finds that the waiver of bond by John is satisfactory. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the court's intent to grant.

Judge: Roger T. Picquet. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.
